core_loop: Engine emits Keys → articulation subscribes to the full stream (wildcard consume) → Tier 1 continuously reframes UI (Concern queue, Memory salience, Bonds/Knot/Belief/Inspiration); Tier 2 matches a Key against 10 trigger conditions, scores significance, fires a 5-15s cut scene or else accumulates "unarticulated weight"; Tier 3 fires annually, selecting top-N Keys peninsula-wide into a chronicle. Pure sink: emits nothing back into mechanics (module_contracts.yaml emits: []).

decision_points.options: player writes 3-5 Belief truth-statements + Inspiration arcs, later revises them
decision_points.note: real authorial choice; engagement adds +2/+1 significance (§3.5), shaping what gets rendered

decision_points.where: §2.1-2.3 Concern queue / Memory salience / Bonds register
decision_points.note: informs choices made in OTHER subsystems (fieldwork/DA); this layer generates zero decisions of its own here, which is appropriate for a renderer but should not be miscounted as a choice surface

north_star.collision_feed: Nothing goes back into mechanics (emits: [] per module_contracts L769) — pure narrative sink. Value is legibility service to OTHER subsystems' collisions (faction/succession/combat coverage is dense and sim-verified), not generation of new choice space here.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star unevenly: strong rendering of faction/political/personal-combat collisions, but silent on Thread collisions and self-contradictory on siege/battle-conclusion coverage — exactly the flagship moments a renderer of emergence exists to catch.

nq.observation: significance() formula restates cleanly after one read; second-order consequence (cut-scene length by score band) is predictable.

Declared as O(1)/queue-based universal subscriber (composes cleanly) but §6.4's claim that scene.battle_concluded 'already triggers Tier 2' is not backed by the §3.1 table — a smoothness violation between two sections of the same canonical doc.
Stage 10 sim (A1-A6) passed 6/6 including determinism (hash match) and cross-faction clustering decision support; this is real verification, not just declared.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Rendering Crisis / Gap manifestation / Dissolution witnessing surfaced as cut scenes
threadwork_junctures.evidence: Zero Thread/Coherence/Calamity/Gap/Dissolution mentions in the file (full-text grep); matches grounding 03's flagged silent juncture verbatim; no dedicated ED ticket found for this specific gap (searched editorial_ledger.jsonl 'articulation' hits: ED-785/841/842/936/1030, none address it) — tag KNOWN-UNTRACKED, not the combat-resolver ED-911.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Victory-condition era transitions (Post-Calamity, Phased Occupation, Anarchy) rendered as chronicle/cut-scene beats
threadwork_junctures.evidence: module_contracts.yaml L679: era transitions are UNKEYED, 'articulation is blind to era changes via the Key stream' — tracked as ED-1006 (open, registry SS10 candidate); I add the sharper framing that this specifically breaks the renderer's own mandate at the game's endgame-adjacent moments.

legibility.note: For factions/NPCs whose Keys intersect one of the 10 §3.1 triggers, Tier 1+2+3 state answers whose-position-at-risk / what-actors-want reasonably well. For actors whose Keys never match a trigger (starvation-accumulator gap, §3.3) or for Thread/Calamity-adjacent threats (zero surfacing), the question 'what happens if no one acts next season' cannot be answered from this subsystem's state alone.

degenerate_candidates: §3.1 trigger table + §5 gating: a Bonded NPC whose Keys never match one of the 10 explicit trigger conditions accumulates unarticulated_weight indefinitely with no code path that forces emission — the anti-starvation promise in §3.3 is unbacked by the actual mechanism, so some NPCs are structurally invisible to Tier 2 forever.

edges.mechanism: universal wildcard subscription to full Key stream
registry-declared consumer; §6.4 claims default Tier-2 trigger

edges.other: faction_politics/npc_behavior (coup, succession, covert_betrayal, strain_shock, cascade_cluster, belief_revised)

edges.mechanism: no Key type exists; declared unreachable

directions.top-down: P-14 requires inseparability expressed in every play mode; the narrative-surfacing layer's total silence on Thread/Calamity events is the sharpest instance of that requirement going unmet.
directions.bottom-up: The significance()/accumulator primitives are well-formed in isolation, but the wiring between them (§3.3's promise vs §5's actual gate) doesn't compose into the claimed anti-starvation behavior.
directions.lateral: Sibling collision-generating subsystems (faction, combat, social) are well covered by the 10-trigger table; siege/battle-conclusion and investigation-verdict Keys, though registry-declared consumers, are not.
directions.diagonal: Cross-scale: peninsula-scale era transitions (GD-1 victory-adjacent) are invisible to the layer whose job is cross-scale narrative rendering.
directions.forwards: Stage 10 sim (6/6 PASS) is real validation infrastructure worth reusing as the model for a future battery covering battle_concluded/investigation_resolved/thread events once those triggers are authored.
directions.backwards: The file carries four disagreeing status markers (CANONICAL x2 vs PROVISIONAL x3) unresolved by CURRENT.md's un-annotated listing — a currency ambiguity in the doc's own header, distinct from any prior ED.

findings.claim: The canonical §3.1 10-trigger ruleset omits scene.battle_concluded and scene.investigation_resolved despite both being registry-declared consumed types, and §6.4 falsely asserts battle_concluded already triggers Tier 2 by an unstated rule.

§3.3's anti-narrative-starvation claim is not implemented by the §5 pseudocode: unarticulated_weight only inflates significance of an already-firing trigger, it cannot itself force a cut scene, so non-trigger-matching Bonded NPCs are permanently un-rendered.

Zero Thread/Coherence/Calamity/Gap/Rendering-Crisis/Dissolution hooks anywhere in the narrative-surfacing layer, despite these being explicitly narratively load-bearing (ED-681); no dedicated ED tracks this specific gap.
findings.tracked_ref: grounding 03 §3 (no dedicated ED found; distinct from combat-resolver ED-911)

findings.claim: Victory-condition era transitions (Post-Calamity, Phased Occupation, Anarchy) are UNKEYED, making the renderer of emergence blind to the game's own endgame-adjacent collision moments.

Articulation emits nothing back into mechanics (chronicle/legend never feeds NPC Renown/memory); a plausible loop exists (npc_behavior Renown/Standing track) with no documented rationale for rejecting it — intent UNDETERMINED.
The file carries four disagreeing status markers (CANONICAL x2, PROVISIONAL x3 incl. footer) and CURRENT.md lists it with no disambiguating annotation, unlike sibling rows.
